verdicts.note: Direction supported (reduce regulatory barriers to build more), but -2 means fully pole_a including skepticism of rent regulation, on which the dossier is silent. A single deregulation plank plus the absence of an investment component cannot carry a full-pole placement; -1 is what the evidence shows.

One pamphlet/site line ('supports law enforcement and accountability for repeat offenders') supports the enforcement lean, but -2 (fully pole_a: staffing, mandatory treatment, clearing encampments) is built partly on what his platform omits. Absence of a prevention plank in a light pamphlet statement is not evidence of full-pole enforcement posture.

Sole evidence is a website education plank ('empower parents; prioritize reading, math, and practical skills'). The axis spans reproductive rights, LGBTQ+ protections, and curricula; a two-word 'empower parents' fragment on one source is indirect inference, not a single clear statement. Low confidence, which the app excludes from alignment math.

The only evidence is the pamphlet phrase 'a tax code that doesn't squeeze working people.' That is compatible with progressive burden-shifting but also with plain tax relief; she names no revenue source and has no cited record on revenue votes. Reading the phrase as pole_b relies on party context — exactly the inference the guide forbids. Direction plausible, confidence low.

Single pamphlet phrase ('workforce housing for the families who serve our communities') with no mechanism — nothing on social housing, rent stabilization, or tenant protections, and nothing against deregulation. The scorer's 'rather than deregulation' basis is absence-based. Keep the lean at +1 only at low confidence.

Explicit pledge to defend reproductive freedom plus Planned Parenthood endorsement supports the direction, but +2 means fully pole_b (reproductive AND gender-affirming care, inclusive schools, anti-discrimination law). The dossier covers only the reproductive-rights element; gun safety is not on this axis. +1 medium is the supportable placement.
